questions.question: What is the TLS named group for X25519 + ML-KEM-768?
How much does hybrid add to TLS handshake size?
questions.explanation: Hybrid key exchange adds approximately 2.3 KB to the handshake.

questions.question: What does the server return in hybrid TLS key exchange?
questions.explanation: Server sends X25519 public key plus ML-KEM ciphertext (not PK).

questions.question: Which RFC covers TLS certificate compression?
questions.explanation: RFC 8879 defines TLS certificate compression to reduce chain sizes.

questions.question: What latency increase should be expected with hybrid TLS?
questions.explanation: Hybrid TLS typically adds 10-30% latency to handshakes.

questions.question: Which browser first deployed hybrid PQC TLS?
questions.explanation: Chrome (via BoringSSL) was first to deploy hybrid PQC TLS at scale.

questions.question: In hybrid TLS, clients must support the hybrid group to connect.
questions.explanation: Clients can fall back to classical groups if they don't support hybrid.
